jokes.text: Singing hologram for Hermes Conrad? Ooh! Fun!
jokes.setup: Leela enthusiastically expects a fun singing telegram
jokes.punchline: It's actually delivering terrible news about Hermes' dying father
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with efficient setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: Oh, rats. I'll just say it. Your father's on his deathbed in Jamaica.
jokes.setup: Hermes expecting a fun singing delivery
jokes.punchline: Hologram gives up on the song format for brutal directness
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of expectations - the singing hologram abandoning its format for blunt delivery is both funny and efficient storytelling

jokes.text: Sorry about your pops, Pops. I didn't realize you had a pops.
jokes.punchline: The play on 'pops' as both nickname and father
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry wordplay - shows his character's simple but endearing way of processing information

jokes.text: How low can it go? Not this low!
jokes.setup: Barometer store owner dealing with hurricane pressure
jokes.punchline: The barometer literally can't measure the devastating pressure that destroys his business
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark irony - the barometer store owner's equipment failing at the worst possible moment is both tragic and absurdly funny

jokes.text: Not the jungle, Daddy! There's green snakeses in there!
jokes.setup: Young Hermes fearing his father going to the jungle
jokes.punchline: The childish pluralization 'snakeses' and innocent fear
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing young Hermes' speech patterns, adds heart while getting a chuckle

jokes.text: But the miserable cruff never did come back.
jokes.setup: Hermes telling the story of his father leaving
jokes.punchline: Using 'cruff' as Jamaican slang insult
jokes.explanation: Character-specific language that shows Hermes' Jamaican heritage while expressing his hurt

jokes.text: Exotic Jamaican crop? - It could be anything. - Step on it, Leela!
jokes.setup: Discussion of what exotic crop Hermes' father might be growing
jokes.punchline: Fry's innocent obliviousness to the marijuana implication, Leela's urgency
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry naivety - everyone else gets the drug reference but he's genuinely clueless

jokes.text: I hope it's pumpkins.
jokes.setup: Local talking about scoring kilos from Hermes' father
jokes.punchline: Fry's continued innocent hope it's pumpkins
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry callback - his pumpkin fixation continues despite increasingly obvious context

jokes.text: Uh... Pumpkins. Medicinal pumpkins.
jokes.setup: Fry learning it's coffee, not drugs
jokes.punchline: His attempt to maintain the pumpkin theory with 'medicinal' modifier
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the running gag - Fry desperately clinging to his pumpkin theory by adding 'medicinal'

jokes.text: I'd say much better than his own children!
jokes.setup: Guide saying Badrick treats his coffee beans like his own children
jokes.punchline: Casually noting he treats them better than his actual child
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective callback to the abandonment theme - casual cruelty delivered as an aside

jokes.text: There you are, ya low-down licky-licky harbor shark!
jokes.setup: Hermes ready to confront his father
jokes.punchline: Creative Jamaican insult chain
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific insult that sounds both authentic and ridiculous - 'licky-licky harbor shark' is memorably absurd

jokes.text: Hang on, I gotta put my hearin' aids in... and turn them on.
jokes.setup: Old man can't hear his son's angry confrontation
jokes.punchline: The two-step process of both inserting and turning on hearing aids
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup - the second beat of turning them on deflates Hermes' momentum

jokes.text: Oops, these my reading glasses. Is there something I'll have to read?
jokes.setup: Badrick struggling to see his son
jokes.punchline: Wrong glasses plus literal interpretation of 'reading my lips'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the physical comedy - literal interpretation of 'read my lips' while wearing wrong glasses

jokes.text: Yeah, my lips! Mmm. Then I'll need my regular glasses.
jokes.setup: Hermes trying to get his father to see him for confrontation
jokes.punchline: Father's methodical approach to vision correction
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the physical comedy bit - deflating dramatic tension with mundane logistics

jokes.text: You look awful!
jokes.setup: Father finally able to see his son after years apart
jokes.punchline: First words are a casual insult about appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - after all the buildup to their reunion, the father's first comment is brutally casual criticism

jokes.text: Though I and I never saw eye-to-eye with him and him, I daren't speak ill of the dead, so, that's all I got.
jokes.punchline: Admits he has nothing good to say but won't speak ill of the dead
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific humor - the Rastafarian speech pattern combined with diplomatic restraint about the deceased

jokes.text: Forty percent steel, baby!
jokes.punchline: Bender's proud declaration of his steel composition
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - makes everything about himself and his metal composition, typical catchphrase usage

jokes.text: Now, to honor Badrick's last cuckoo banana wishes, he gonna be buried in a burlap coffee sack.
jokes.punchline: 'Cuckoo banana wishes' and coffee sack burial
jokes.explanation: 'Cuckoo banana' is a fun character-specific phrase, and the coffee sack burial fits the theme

jokes.text: I feel the same way. You've been a father to me.
jokes.setup: Hermes saying Professor has been more of a father than his real dad
jokes.punchline: Professor reversing it - Hermes has been a father to him
jokes.explanation: Sweet absurdist moment - the age reversal is both heartwarming and ridiculous given their actual ages

jokes.text: Good thing I always carry my pocket gravedigger. At my age, you never know when you'll need it.
jokes.punchline: Professor casually carrying a pocket gravedigger for morbid practical reasons
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor perfectly suited to the Professor's character - morbid pragmatism delivered casually

jokes.text: No, let's wait until I'm older.
jokes.setup: Should they investigate the mysterious hatch?
jokes.punchline: Professor wanting to wait until he's older despite being ancient
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor absurdism - being over 100 but still wanting to wait until he's older shows his detachment from reality

jokes.text: Sweet UFO of Tupelo! It's a spaceship! And not of this Earth. It must have come from space.
jokes.punchline: Stating increasingly obvious facts about space origin
jokes.explanation: Typical Professor over-explanation of obvious facts, plus the Elvis reference in the exclamation

jokes.text: More amazing than the fact you licked the drippings off an alien rocket pipe?
jokes.punchline: Leela pointing out he just licked alien rocket drippings
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction highlighting the gross absurdity of what the Professor just did

jokes.text: Stop calling everything 'weird.' You should get out more.
jokes.punchline: Professor telling others to get out more while in an alien spaceship
jokes.explanation: Ironic advice from someone who's lived an incredibly adventurous life, delivered in the most exotic situation possible

jokes.text: All of the world's most important sandal-wearing cultures!
jokes.punchline: Categorizing them by sandal-wearing instead of coffee
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - the Professor finds a completely different pattern than the obvious coffee connection

jokes.text: If you come across their half-and-half, don't open it.
jokes.punchline: Warning about equally ancient dairy products
jokes.explanation: Good practical joke about ancient dairy - the gross-out factor is well-earned and relatable

jokes.text: Coffee? Stop tasting stuff!
jokes.punchline: Leela's exasperated command to stop
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leela's frustration with the Professor's reckless tasting behavior

jokes.text: You drank it? Gross!
jokes.punchline: Leela's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat - straightforward disgust at the Professor's actions

jokes.text: I'm seeing every color of the coffee rainbow! Me, too! It's a double mocha hallucaccino!
jokes.punchline: 'Hallucaccino' portmanteau and coffee rainbow
jokes.explanation: Creative portmanteau combining hallucination with cappuccino, plus the absurd 'coffee rainbow' concept

jokes.text: No way, man. We're just whacked out of our gourds!
jokes.setup: Wondering if aliens came back to life
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual dismissal using drug slang
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's street-smart side with drug terminology

jokes.text: We're aliens, not perverts!
jokes.punchline: Treating decaf as morally worse than being an alien
jokes.explanation: Brilliant hierarchy of moral outrage - being an alien is acceptable but making decaf crosses a line. Perfect economy and unexpectedness.

jokes.text: The first and only good use of tea.
jokes.punchline: Dismissing all other uses of tea
jokes.explanation: Good coffee supremacist humor from the alien - dismisses tea entirely except for its destruction

jokes.text: Tea!
jokes.punchline: Professor's disgusted exclamation
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat - Professor sharing the alien's tea disgust

jokes.text: Mayday! Mayday! Coffee crotch!
jokes.punchline: Coffee spill in unfortunate location causing crash
jokes.explanation: Absurd cause for cosmic tragedy - 'coffee crotch' is both funny and explains the crash that doomed humanity

jokes.text: People will think we're crazy. Crazier!
jokes.setup: Leela saying people will think they're crazy
jokes.punchline: Professor's correction acknowledging their baseline craziness
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about the characters' already questionable mental state

jokes.text: Sorry, it's cold, but that's how you serve revenge.
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'revenge is a dish best served cold'
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on the classic revenge saying, applied to both coffee temperature and emotional payload

jokes.text: Maybe we could plant them. We're alive! Oh, you flatter me.
jokes.setup: Fry suggesting they could plant the beans since they're alive
jokes.punchline: Professor taking it as a compliment about his vitality
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Professor pleased to be considered definitively alive at his advanced age

jokes.text: That way, if he's still alive, he can hack his way out.
jokes.setup: Glad Hermes buried the hatchet with his father
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation with morbid implication
jokes.explanation: Dark humor taking the burial hatchet literally - casual consideration of premature burial

jokes.text: It takes you an hour to make instant. Zing!
jokes.punchline: Pointing out he's terrible at even instant coffee, plus self-awareness with 'Zing!'
jokes.explanation: Good character-consistent joke about Hermes' domestic incompetence, with self-aware 'zing' tag

jokes.text: This is more important than you know, but I'll be home soon, son.
jokes.setup: Hermes staying to run the farm
jokes.punchline: Echoing his father's exact abandonment promise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing Hermes repeating the cycle - the exact same words his father used to abandon him

jokes.text: Don't we deliver things, or something?
jokes.setup: Planet Express becoming a coffee company
jokes.punchline: Fry vaguely remembering their actual business
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about the show's premise delivered in typical Fry uncertain style

jokes.text: Light, medium, and, bendissimo!
jokes.punchline: Adding his own name to create 'bendissimo'
jokes.explanation: Good Bender ego integration - turning 'fortissimo' into a self-referential coffee term

jokes.text: Ah. That's a damn fine bath of coffee.
jokes.punchline: Twin Peaks reference from Kyle MacLachlan himself
jokes.explanation: Meta celebrity cameo referencing MacLachlan's Twin Peaks coffee obsession - high nostalgia value

jokes.text: Outta my ways, Kwisatz Haderachses. Fill'ers up, coffee jerk!
jokes.punchline: Dune reference with typical Bender rudeness
jokes.explanation: Bender being characteristically rude while dropping sci-fi references

jokes.text: Meaning a half-caf, teeny-weeny-mocha-cheenie with a plop-top of froth foam and do not even think of whipping that dollop.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous coffee terminology ending with dollop threat
jokes.explanation: Good Amy character moment with made-up coffee terms and her typical demanding attitude

jokes.text: Because you look like a dollop-whipper.
jokes.setup: Amy's concern about whipping the dollop
jokes.punchline: Accusatory character assessment based on appearance
jokes.explanation: Amy's paranoid character trait - judging people's dollop-whipping tendencies by appearance

jokes.text: I've got the energy of 10 fiddler crabs.
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to fiddler crabs
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd comparison that sounds both Jamaican and scientific

jokes.text: Ow! It's almost boiling. What room is that temperature? This one, sugarpuss. 'Cause you're heating it up right now.
jokes.setup: Customer complaining about room temperature coffee
jokes.punchline: Fry's smooth pickup line explanation
jokes.explanation: Fry attempting smooth talk with questionable success - typical character romantic incompetence

jokes.text: If it's my faults the Earth rotates, then it's the Earth's faults I did thises!
jokes.setup: Scruffy angry about new day meaning new coffee charge
jokes.punchline: Blaming the Earth's rotation for his actions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy logic - blaming cosmic forces for personal responsibility in his distinctive speech pattern

jokes.text: Not me, baby. I'm equipment.
jokes.punchline: Bender's literal self-classification
jokes.explanation: Great Bender moment - matter-of-fact about his robot status while avoiding responsibility

jokes.text: Last night, he asked if you're having an affair. It was the first good laugh we've had since you left.
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of Hermes having an affair being genuinely funny
jokes.explanation: Dark but affectionate humor about Hermes being so unlikely to have an affair that it's genuinely amusing

jokes.text: We share a child. Yes, yes, but nothing of value.
jokes.setup: LaBarbara pointing out they share a child
jokes.punchline: Hermes dismissing their son as valueless
jokes.explanation: Brutal dark humor - Hermes casually dismissing his son's value in corporate terms

jokes.text: How about a silent but deadly partner?
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's fart reference mixed with business terminology
jokes.explanation: Typical Zoidberg lowbrow humor - mixing business terms with bathroom jokes

jokes.text: When I had you, I should have aimed for the trash!
jokes.setup: Mom saying she aims for the sky
jokes.punchline: Wishing she had aimed differently when giving birth
jokes.explanation: Classic Mom cruelty - turning her business advice into a brutal personal attack on Walt

jokes.text: They'll spread across the world like steaming hot acne.
jokes.punchline: Comparing franchises to acne spread
jokes.explanation: Vivid and slightly disgusting comparison that captures both the spread pattern and Mom's cynical view

jokes.text: You burn the beans enough, it's like drinking caffeinated cigar ashes.
jokes.setup: Explaining how to hide bad coffee quality
jokes.punchline: Unappetizing but accurate description
jokes.explanation: Good observation about bad coffee with disgusting but accurate comparison

jokes.text: We will triple-never double-ever disrespect this coffee!
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top grammatical construction
jokes.explanation: Great Hermes speech pattern showing his passionate dedication

jokes.text: We'll stick with the plan. No one messes with my shrubs.
jokes.punchline: Referring to coffee plants as shrubs
jokes.explanation: Hermes' protective attitude toward his coffee plants, calling them shrubs in his practical way

jokes.text: Weird. This one looks almost like my father. Zoidberg, you're doing topiary? Nopiary!
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's 'nopiary' pun denial
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on topiary, plus sets up the father's return - good visual comedy setup

jokes.text: You're scared o'shrubs? I really was a bad father.
jokes.punchline: Father's guilt over son being afraid of plants
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the father's misplaced guilt and inability to understand normal reactions

jokes.text: I mean, get a cup holder!
jokes.setup: Discussing the alien coffee spill that crashed the ship
jokes.punchline: Practical solution to cosmic tragedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect practical dad response to cosmic tragedy - reducing galactic catastrophe to simple automotive safety

jokes.text: But now, all I want to say to you is... I'm good at this!
jokes.setup: Hermes about to forgive his father
jokes.punchline: Instead brags about his gravedigging skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - builds up emotional reconciliation then deflates to mundane pride in gravedigging ability

jokes.text: The reason is, I was a dingdong!
jokes.setup: Hermes explaining why he won't come home
jokes.punchline: Using his father's self-deprecating term
jokes.explanation: Good callback to father's self-assessment, showing Hermes recognizing the same pattern in himself

jokes.text: Why won't Pops come home? Did I do something to disappoint him? Plenty. But that's not the reason.
jokes.punchline: Mother's brutally honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent brutal honesty with perfect comedic timing

jokes.text: Sorry, I ain'ts had my coffees today. Owses!
jokes.punchline: Characteristic speech pattern plus implied physical mishap
jokes.explanation: Classic Scruffy moment - his distinctive speech pattern combined with coffee dependency

jokes.text: I already resold the whole crap load for a butt-truck of ass-cash!
jokes.setup: Revelation that Mom resold the coffee farm
jokes.punchline: Mom's crude but colorful description of the profit
jokes.explanation: Typical Mom vulgarity with creative compound curse words - 'butt-truck of ass-cash' is memorably crude

jokes.text: Pops, aren't I a little old to be sitting on your lap? And heavy, too. But stay just a little longer.
jokes.setup: Adult son sitting on father's lap
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging awkwardness but wanting the moment anyway
jokes.explanation: Sweet and awkward father-son moment - acknowledges the physical absurdity while maintaining emotional sincerity

jokes.text: Thanks to the holy bean, humanity will soon be elevated to its final subjugation.
jokes.punchline: Revealing the elevation is actually enslavement
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark twist - 'elevated to subjugation' is a perfect oxymoron revealing the aliens' true sinister purpose
